implementation_hypotheses: CloudBase physical-space canonical state uses database transactions so concurrent function instances retry read-merge-write conflicts
mobile remote authentication uses challenge-bound SMS verification, short-lived access tokens, rotating refresh tokens, and revocable server sessions
mobile refresh is demand-driven before protected requests and serialized per session so one refresh token is never submitted concurrently

an authenticated v2 bootstrap read derives account identity from the active session and returns membership, daily progress, learning card state, nullable scheduler cursor, physical space, and content version without accepting phone_number input
missing bootstrap components return explicit empty server state and never import device-local state; CloudBase response freshness is explicit and does not claim a cross-collection transaction
bootstrap content version is derived from normalized ordered content; production fails closed without a matching published content-release.v1 descriptor while development may expose a null release id

mobile remote login and restored sessions validate bootstrap scope before hydrating its canonical baseline, then validate the loaded card-source identity and content version before enabling learning, persisting reconciled state, replaying mutations, or pushing product-state writes

mobile keeps a still-valid authenticated session retryable after transient bootstrap failure but fails closed without previously validated canonical state and required content; reconnect reads canonical state before replay and again after replay
bootstrap does not make legacy snapshot writes, server scheduling, idempotent learning events, signed content manifests, or the CommonJS CloudBase function production-ready
development-only v1 product routes can accept an active v2 session during migration while production continues to reject every v1 route
remote content failures fail closed and never substitute bundled development cards

auth_session_store.non_sensitive_revocation_marker: versioned_AsyncStorage tombstone is written before secure credential cleanup and blocks restoration until a new secure session is saved
auth_session_store.legacy_migration: auth-session.v1 cannot be upgraded without refresh credentials and is invalidated instead of restored
auth_session_store.refresh_policy: refresh within 60 seconds of access expiry, serialize concurrent refresh demand, persist the complete rotated pair before exposing it, and never retry a rejected refresh token
auth_session_store.authorization_rejection: refresh rejection, repeated HTTP 401, or HTTP 403 clears account-bound persistence and returns to login

user_state_store.migration: user-state.v1 space entries receive an epoch timestamp so stale restored state cannot outrank server canonical state
user_state_store.space_state_timestamp: preserve the timestamp of each explicit favorite or sleep action

local_persistence.mutation_queue: persisted_under_an_independent_key_and_not_embedded_in_user_state; persisted mutation payloads and identifiers contain no credentials, context contains phone_number only, legacy credential-bearing membership identifiers are normalized during hydration, and the current access token is injected in memory during replay
local_persistence.logout: attempt server session revocation first, then write the local auth revocation marker and clear secure auth, user state, and account-bound mutation queue before presenting logged-out runtime state; local cleanup still completes when the server is unavailable
